CWFSJU.office: National Weather Service San Juan PR

HSFAT2.office: NWS National Hurricane Center Miami FL
HSFAT2.synopsis: Atlantic High Seas Forecast covering 7N-31N west of 35W including Caribbean Sea and Gulf of America.

OFFNT4.office: NWS National Hurricane Center Miami, FL
OFFNT4.synopsis: A stationary front extends across the Florida Straits to the Yucatan Channel. This front will weaken into tonight then dissipate by Tue. Moderate to fresh SE to S return flow will set up in the W Gulf Tue night into Thu as high pressure builds into the eastern Gulf. By the end of the week, this high will settle over the entire basin, leading to tranquil conditions for regional waters.

OFFNT1.office: NWS Ocean Prediction Center Washington DC

OFFNT3.office: NWS National Hurricane Center Miami, FL
OFFNT3.synopsis: The pressure gradient between the Bermuda High and the Colombian Low will support fresh to strong trades over the S central Caribbean, and moderate to fresh trades over the remainder of the E and central Caribbean this week. The remnants of a frontal boundary will drift across the NW Caribbean in Tue. SE winds over the Gulf of Honduras will increase to strong speeds Tue through Fri.

CWFBRO.synopsis: Favorable marine conditions expected today into tonight. The pressure gradient strengthens Tuesday into Wednesday with increased winds and briefly elevated seas. A frontal system arrives late week with another round of potential adverse conditions with showers and thunderstorms.

OFFNT2.office: NWS Ocean Prediction Center Washington DC

CWFCRP.office: National Weather Service Corpus Christi TX

CWFLCH.office: National Weather Service Lake Charles LA

CWFLIX.office: National Weather Service New Orleans LA

CWFMOB.synopsis: A southerly flow will develop this afternoon and increase Tuesday into Wednesday ahead of a cold front. A light northerly flow develops late in the week behind the front.

CWFTAE.synopsis: Light to gentle northeasterly winds are forecast across the northeastern Gulf today thanks to high pressure overhead. Light to moderate southerly winds develop later tonight and last through at least Wednesday before turning more southwesterly Thursday ahead of another cold front along with a few showers and storms Thursday into Thursday night.

CWFTBW.office: National Weather Service Tampa Bay Ruskin FL
CWFTBW.synopsis: High pressure will build over the waters today and tonight with winds and seas continuing to subside. The area of high pressure will hold over the region through the remainder of the week with relatively benign conditions expected.

BDM.synopsis: Outlook: Seas will subside across the island chain on Tuesday while unsettled weather persists across the Northwest and Central Bahamas as the weakening front slowly drifts south. The front is expected to reach the Southeast Bahamas by Tuesday evening before exiting the country by Wednesday. More stable conditions will return by late Wednesday as high pressure builds across the area. However, another weak front is anticipated to approach the

AMZ700.name: 352 AM AST Mon May 4 2026
AMZ700.raw:
.Synopsis for Puerto Rico and the U.S. Virgin Islands Waters...
A broad high pressure over the central Atlantic will maintain an
easterly flow over the next few days. Winds will remain generally
gentle to moderate, around 10 to 15 knots, with a few periods of
locally fresh conditions tonight into Monday. Seas will range
between 3 and 5 feet, occasionally up to 6 feet due to a combination
of local wind waves and a small, long-period northeasterly swell.
Overall conditions will remain favorable for small craft, though
operators should exercise caution at times due to wind-driven seas
and passing showers that may produce locally higher winds and
seas. A gradual improvement is expected later in the week as winds
ease slightly and seas subside.

AMZ600.name: 356 AM EDT Mon May 4 2026
AMZ600.raw:
.Synopsis for Jupiter Inlet to Ocean Reef FL out to 60 nm and for
East Cape Sable to Bonita Beach FL out to 60 nm...

Surface winds will veer to a northeasterly direction after daybreak
across all local waters as a stationary frontal boundary remains to
our south over the Florida Straits. Winds will become breezy after
daybreak with gusty winds in particular forecast to increase across
the northern Atlantic waters zones as a pressure gradient briefly
enhances. A Small Craft Advisory is in effect for the northern
Atlantic waters from 8am to 5pm today. Any showers and thunderstorms
that develop across local waters today may result in locally
elevated hazardous wave and winds. Winds will gradually lessen and
veer more easterly in direction on Tuesday.

Gulf Stream Hazards: Gusty winds around 20 knots with higher gusts
possible across the northern Atlantic waters.

The approximate location of the west wall of the Gulf Stream as of
Apr 29, 2026.

  8 nautical miles north northeast of Fowey Rocks.
  7 nautical miles east of Port Everglades.
 10 nautical miles east southeast of Lake Worth.
 11 nautical miles east northeast of Jupiter Inlet.

This data courtesy of the Naval Oceanographic Office.

AMZ650.name: Coastal waters from Jupiter Inlet to Deerfield Beach FL out 20 NM

periods.label: TODAY
periods.text: NE winds 15 to 20 kt with gusts up to 25 kt. Seas 5 to 7 ft, occasionally to 9 ft. Wave Detail: NE 6 ft at 7 seconds. Intracoastal waters choppy in exposed areas. A chance of tstms.

periods.label: TONIGHT
periods.text: E winds 15 to 20 kt, diminishing to 10 to 15 kt after midnight. Seas 5 to 7 ft, occasionally to 9 ft, subsiding to 3 to 5 ft, occasionally to 6 ft after midnight. Wave Detail: NE 6 ft at 7 seconds and NW 3 ft at 8 seconds, becoming E 4 ft at 7 seconds and N 3 ft at 7 seconds. Intracoastal waters choppy in exposed areas. A chance of tstms in the evening. A chance of showers.

periods.label: TUE
periods.text: E winds around 10 kt. Seas 2 to 4 ft, occasionally to 5 ft. Wave Detail: E 3 ft at 6 seconds and N 2 ft at 7 seconds. Intracoastal waters light chop. A chance of showers.

periods.label: TUE NIGHT
periods.text: E winds around 10 kt. Seas 2 to 3 ft. Wave Detail: N 2 ft at 7 seconds and E 1 foot at 4 seconds. Intracoastal waters light chop. A chance of showers in the evening.

periods.label: WED AND WED NIGHT
periods.text: SE winds around 10 kt. Seas around 2 ft. Wave Detail: SE 1 foot at 4 seconds and N 1 foot at 7 seconds. Intracoastal waters light chop.

periods.text: S winds 10 to 15 kt. Seas around 2 ft. Wave Detail: S 1 foot at 3 seconds and N 1 foot at 6 seconds. Intracoastal waters light chop.

periods.label: FRI NIGHT
periods.text: SE winds 10 to 15 kt. Seas around 2 ft. Intracoastal waters light chop. A chance of showers after midnight. Winds and seas higher in and near tstms.

ANZ898.name: 1032 AM EDT Mon May 4 2026

periods.label: SYNOPSIS FOR NEW ENGLAND WATERS
periods.text: Intense low pressure NE of the waters, will continue moving NE away from the area through tonight. A weak high pressure ridge will move E over the region today, then gradually shift E of the area tonight. A cold front will approach the waters from the W Tue, then stall just W of the area Tue night into Wed night as low pressure tracks NE along the boundary. The cold front will move into the waters Thu and Thu night as yet another low tracks NE along the boundary. The front will push E of the waters Fri followed by a secondary cold front or low pressure trough later Fri. High pressure will build across the area Fri night behind the secondary front or trough.

ANZ898.raw:
.SYNOPSIS FOR NEW ENGLAND WATERS...Intense low pressure NE of the
waters, will continue moving NE away from the area through
tonight. A weak high pressure ridge will move E over the region
today, then gradually shift E of the area tonight. A cold front
will approach the waters from the W Tue, then stall just W of the
area Tue night into Wed night as low pressure tracks NE along the
boundary. The cold front will move into the waters Thu and Thu
night as yet another low tracks NE along the boundary. The front
will push E of the waters Fri followed by a secondary cold front
or low pressure trough later Fri. High pressure will build across
the area Fri night behind the secondary front or trough.

ANZ899.name: 1032 AM EDT Mon May 4 2026

periods.label: SYNOPSIS FOR MID ATLC WATERS
periods.text: Intense low pressure NE of the waters will continue moving NE of the area through tonight. High pressure will gradually shift E of the region today through Tue night while maintaining a ridge across the area. The ridge will weaken Wed and Wed night as a cold front slowly approaches from the W. The cold front will move SE into the waters Thu and Thu night as developing low pressure tracks N and NE from the northern waters. A portion of the front will stall across the far S waters late Fri and low pressure will track NE along the front Fri night. High pressure will build offshore across the waters N of the stalled front Fri and Fri night.

ANZ899.raw:
.SYNOPSIS FOR MID ATLC WATERS...Intense low pressure NE of the
waters will continue moving NE of the area through tonight. High
pressure will gradually shift E of the region today through Tue
night while maintaining a ridge across the area. The ridge will
weaken Wed and Wed night as a cold front slowly approaches from
the W. The cold front will move SE into the waters Thu and Thu
night as developing low pressure tracks N and NE from the
northern waters. A portion of the front will stall across the far
S waters late Fri and low pressure will track NE along the front
Fri night. High pressure will build offshore across the waters N
of the stalled front Fri and Fri night.

GMZ200.name: 231 AM CDT Mon May 4 2026

periods.label: SYNOPSIS FOR THE MIDDLE TEXAS COASTAL WATERS
periods.text: A gentle to moderate (BF 3-4) onshore breeze will increase to a moderate to fresh (BF 4-5) breeze through the middle of the week. Winds will shift briefly to the northeast late Thursday through Friday night as a cold front moves across the area before returning to the southeast Saturday. A low to medium chance of showers and thunderstorms will return Wednesday and continue through the weekend.

GMZ200.raw:
.SYNOPSIS FOR THE MIDDLE TEXAS COASTAL WATERS...

A gentle to moderate (BF 3-4) onshore breeze will increase to a
moderate to fresh (BF 4-5) breeze through the middle of the week.
Winds will shift briefly to the northeast late Thursday through
Friday night as a cold front moves across the area before returning
to the southeast Saturday. A low to medium chance of showers and
thunderstorms will return Wednesday and continue through the
weekend.

GMZ300.name: 1217 PM CDT Mon May 4 2026
GMZ300.raw:
.Synopsis For High Island to the Matagorda Ship Channel Out 60 NM
including Galveston and Matagorda Bays.

South to southeast winds 15 to 20 knots will prevail through
midweek, The next cold front, and isolated to scattered showers
or thunderstorms, is forecast to push off the coast Thursday
morning. This front does not appear to be as strong in terms of
wind speeds compared to the last two. North and northeast winds
behind the front swing back around to the southeast by Friday and
continue into the weekend. Additional rain chances are expected
this weekend in association with a slow moving upper level
disturbance moving into the region from the west.

GMZ400.name: 309 AM CDT Mon May 4 2026
GMZ400.raw:
.Synopsis for Lower Atchafalaya River LA to High Island TX out
60 NM including Sabine and Calcasieu Lakes and Vermilion and Cote
Blanche Bays...

Onshore winds will become reestablished today between 10-15 knots
where they`ll remain until Thursday morning. A cold front is expected
to move through the coastal waters Thursday morning accompanied by
showers and thunderstorms that are likely to continue following the
frontal passage through at least Friday morning. Breezy offshore flow
between 15-20 knots will develop behind the front Thursday into Friday
before turning onshore again next weekend.

GMZ500.name: 537 AM CDT Mon May 4 2026

periods.label: SYNOPSIS FOR PASCAGOULA TO ATCHAFALAYA RIVER OUT 60 NM
periods.text: High pressure will continue to settle over the waters today resulting in light winds and calm seas. As the high pulls to the east on Tuesday, winds will turn southerly and increase to 10 to 15 knots. Further increases to 15 to 20 knots or exercise caution conditions will occur on Wednesday as a low pressure system passes to the north of the area. A weak front will slip into the waters and stall over the region on Thursday leading to more variable winds of 10 to 15 knots. By Friday, the weak front will dissipate and southeast winds of 10 to 15 knots will return.
